page: Amazon Web Services x2idn.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features Intel Xeon 8375C processors, providing a total of 64 physical cores and 128 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 2048.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 16.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, the instance includes 3800 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a 100 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the x2idn.metal demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for the stress-ng multi-core CPU test and PassMark CPU score. Single-core performance is moderate, ranking in the middle 50% for stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core tests. Memory bandwidth is strong, with read-only small block cached memory bandwidth reaching 6746.43 GB/sec and large block uncached read bandwidth at 334.29 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Database operations and Redis SET operations also perform in the top 25%, with Redis handling 113.64 million operations per second.

Given its bare-metal classification and massive 2 TB memory capacity, this instance represents a high-density resource profile. The qualitative cost efficiency is centered on workloads requiring massive memory-to-CPU ratios and high-throughput networking, where virtualized overhead must be avoided. However, the tradeoff lies in its average single-threaded performance, indicating it is less optimal for single-core bound tasks. The hardware configuration and benchmark results make it suitable for memory-intensive applications, large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services x2idn.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 2048.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests, alongside strong memory bandwidth and database operations. Conversely, single-core performance remains in the middle 50%. This high-density hardware profile offers high memory-to-CPU ratios, making it suitable for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large-scale databases, and high-throughput in-memory caching systems like Redis, where bare-metal access minimizes virtualization overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2idn.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 2048 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and strong memory bandwidth performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2idn.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 128 vCPUs, 2 TB DDR4 RAM, 3.8 TB NVMe SSD, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized platform featuring two terabytes of RAM, high-speed NVMe storage, and top-tier multi-core processing for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores via Intel Xeon 8375C processors
2048 GB of DDR4 memory providing 16 GB of RAM per physical core
3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 100 Gbps network bandwidth
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and strong memory bandwidth
Suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads
